# Phase 9.2.4 Governance Consolidation Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, or deployment authority.

## Objective

Consolidate all governance doctrine, survivability philosophy, skepticism lineage, restraint architecture, anti-escalation safeguards, constitutional continuity, archival permanence, fail-closed evidence, anti-capture philosophy, uncertainty governance, and anti-drift safeguards into a unified civilization-governance survivability framework.

## Scope

- Civilization governance consolidation doctrine.
- Unified survivability architecture.
- Constitutional continuity consolidation.
- Governance coherence consolidation.
- Restraint survivability consolidation.
- Anti-escalation safeguard consolidation.
- Civilization permanence integration.
- Long-horizon survivability integration.
- Constitutional framework unification.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No consolidation-review system creates runtime authority.
- No framework-unification process bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No survivability-synthesis layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No governance-convergence layer creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No consolidation framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows constitutional freeze and long-horizon preservation.
- Safe to include now: unified framework synthesis and source-doctrine preservation rules.
- Future upgrade: tribunal evidence admissibility, docket integrity, and record authenticity review.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, API integrations, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
